action.reasoning: Observation: the frozen CLIP+MLP probe can now overfit 64 samples to 25% accuracy, so the pipeline finally learns, but still far from enough for full-task performance. Analysis: the next highest-value step is to scale this recovered pipeline to full data with a stronger MLP head, class-balanced loss, and frozen backbone first; this is much safer than reintroducing full finetuning before we have a non-random full-data baseline. We also need a complete, non-truncated train.py because the current workspace copy is truncated. Decision: rewrite train.py cleanly around the working frozen-CLIP MLP probe, train a short full-data run for 3 epochs with weighted loss and validation by worst-region accuracy, smoke-test on val via predict.py, and submit eval if local val is clearly non-random.
action.command: python train.py --data_dir /data --epochs 3 --batch_size 256 --num_workers 8 --lr 3e-3 --weight_decay 1e-4 --dropout 0.2 --hidden_dim 1024 --freeze_backbone --checkpoint_path checkpoints/strong_v1.pt --last_checkpoint_path checkpoints/strong_v1_last.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/strong_v1.pt --eval_split val --batch_size 256 --num_workers 8
